query: CRT television static screen flicker old TV monitor
message: anti-aesthetic / clarity_and_focus / analog_degradation: CRT television static and old TV monitors

response.objective_caption: A close-up photograph shows a floral patterned surface in the foreground, featuring stylized flowers in green, orange, yellow, purple, and brown with dark green leaves on a textured light background. Behind this surface, an old CRT television displays a pale pink-purple screen with a small bright white reflection or glare spot near the top center and a slightly lighter horizontal band across the lower portion of the screen. The television sits in a dimly lit room with dark surroundings barely visible at the edges. The floral surface appears to be a tablecloth, tray liner, or similar domestic textile with a retro decorative pattern.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A close-up photograph shows a floral patterned surface in the foreground, featuring stylized flowers in green, orange, yellow, purple, and brown with dark green leaves on a textured light background. Behind this surface, an old CRT television displays a degraded, muted pink-purple screen with visible noise, scanlines, and analog texture characteristic of vintage tube television technology, with a small bright white reflection near the top center and a washed-out horizontal band across the lower portion. The pale, faded tones of the screen blend with the dim, suffocating darkness of the surrounding room, creating an atmosphere of obsolete technology and faded domestic nostalgia.
